background: How large a sample is needed to test the oat bran data presented earlier in the chapter with 80% power at α=0.05 two sided? We want to detect a mean change of 0.2 mmol/L and will assume a standard deviation of 0.4 mmol/L.

task.query: Calculate the required sample size using the given parameters: mean change of 0.2 mmol/L, standard deviation of 0.4 mmol/L, power of 80%, and α=0.05 (two-sided).
task.answer: n=σ²(z₀.₈+z₀.₉₇₅)²/300²=0.4²(0.84+1.96)²/300²=31.36. Round up to 32 to ensure adequate power.

task.query: Determine if an adjustment factor f is needed when the sample size exceeds 30.
task.answer: Because the sample size exceeds 30, there is no need to apply adjustment factor f.

meta_info.caption: Sample size requirement, paired-t test (Oat bran).
